evidence: plugin.json contains name, description, author but no version field; Claude Code marketplace requires version for registration and versioned installs
description: plugin.json is missing the required version field, preventing marketplace publication and versioned plugin resolution
suggested_fix: Add "version": "0.1.0" field alongside name and description in plugin.json

evidence: Line 31: 'If significant changes detected' — 'significant' is an unanchored vague quantifier with no numeric or structural threshold defined
description: Vague quantifier 'significant' in update-mode step 1 leaves change detection threshold undefined for the orchestrating agent
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete threshold such as 'If 5 or more files changed, or any file under src/ changed'

evidence: Line 109: 'Notable dependencies' in subagent prompt template — 'notable' is a subjective, unanchored qualifier
description: Vague quantifier 'notable' in the example subagent prompt template leaves dependency scope undefined
suggested_fix: Replace with 'Direct imports and significant transitive dependencies (depth 1-2)'

description: Vague term 'non-obvious' appears in subagent prompt template and output template sections; subjective without definition
suggested_fix: Replace with 'undocumented assumptions, side effects, or behaviors that differ from what the function or file name implies'

evidence: Line 26: curl-pipe-sh string appears inside print(..., file=sys.stderr); grep confirms no subprocess/os.system call anywhere in the file; the script never executes the command
description: Script outputs curl-pipe-sh installation advice in its stderr error message but does not execute it; recommends an insecure install pattern to users
fp_reason: The curl|sh pattern is inside a sys.stderr print() statement used as user-facing advisory text, not executable code; the script has no shell execution surface
rule_gap: SEC-curl-pipe-sh should distinguish print/log-only occurrences from actual subprocess/os.system/eval execution to avoid CRITICAL false positives on help-text strings
suggested_fix: Replace the curl-pipe-sh recommendation with 'pip install tiktoken' (already shown two lines below) or a versioned release URL

evidence: Line 53: 'pip install tiktoken' with no version specifier; same pattern repeated at line 291
description: SKILL.md instructs the orchestrating agent to run pip install tiktoken without a version constraint, allowing arbitrary future versions including breaking or malicious releases
suggested_fix: Pin to a known-good range: pip install 'tiktoken>=0.7,<1.0'

evidence: Line 4: dependencies = ["tiktoken"] in PEP 723 inline script spec with no version constraint
description: Inline UV script dependency 'tiktoken' carries no version pin; uv will resolve to the latest release on every cold run
suggested_fix: Change to "tiktoken>=0.7,<1.0" in the inline dependencies array
